season.league: NICA Utah High School Cycling League

roles.name: Practice Planning & Skills Lead
roles.summary: Weekly practice plans · Monthly skill themes · Season arc.
description: The flow and progression of our Tuesday/Thursday 6–8am practices throughout the season, plus the monthly skill themes that give structure to our development.
You'll own monthly theme development, practice structure, and communicate the season arc to coaches and families.

roles.name: Ride Leader Coordinator
roles.summary: Parent volunteer training · Weekly assignments · Certifications.
description: Making sure every practice group has the right ride leaders, and helping parent volunteers feel prepared and confident in their role.
You'll manage ride leader assignments, track certifications, and run a pre-season training for new parent volunteers.

roles.name: Race Weekend Captain
roles.timeCommitment: 5–10 hrs per race weekend (3 races) + planning
roles.summary: Race day logistics · Warm-ups · Staging · Playbook.
description: Making race weekends run smoothly so riders can focus on racing and having fun.
You'll build warm-up protocols, a staging system, race day role assignments, and communicate expectations to coaches and families.

roles.name: Communication & Culture Lead
roles.summary: Family emails · Social media · Recruiting materials · Team culture.
description: Keeping families informed and engaged, plus building a team culture where character development actually happens.
You'll handle regular family communications, recruiting materials, social media, and plan team culture moments throughout the season.

roles.name: Registration & Roster Manager
roles.timeCommitment: 10–15 hrs in April · 2–3 hrs/month during season
roles.summary: Pit Zone registration · Roster management · Practice-ready verification.
description: Making sure everyone is properly registered, rostered, and practice-ready with the league. No one practices or races until they're properly registered.
You'll manage Pit Zone, track registration completion, follow up with families, and maintain a cleared-to-practice list.

roles.name: Ride Group Coordinator
roles.timeCommitment: 5–8 hrs in May · 2–3 hrs/month for adjustments
roles.summary: New group naming system · Time trials · Rider placement.
description: A ride group system that makes sense to everyone and helps riders find their right level. Last year's color system created more confusion than clarity — you get to solve that.
You'll design the group naming/structure, run time trials in early practices, place riders, and adjust groups throughout the season.

roles.name: Safety & First Aid Lead
description: Keeping everyone safe and knowing what to do when accidents happen. Parents trust us with their kids — you protect that trust.
You'll handle safety reminders, first aid at practices and races, injury documentation, and walk coaches through protocols.

roles.name: Finance / Treasurer
roles.summary: Budget & financial reporting · Registration fees · Fundraising coordination.
description: Team budget, registration fees, fundraising coordination, and financial reporting. Every dollar you manage helps get kids on bikes.
You'll create the season budget, track expenditures, process team fees, handle reimbursements, and deliver a year-end financial report.

roles.name: Equipment & Gear Manager
roles.summary: Loaner bikes · Radios · First aid supplies · Trailer · Race setup.
description: Team equipment, trailer, loaner bikes, radios, first aid supplies, and making sure we have what we need for practices and races.
You'll maintain inventory, organize the trailer, handle loaner bike checkout, and get everything to practice and race venues.

roles.name: Fundraising & Sponsorship Lead
roles.summary: Sponsor development · Fundraising events · Grant applications.
description: Bringing in resources beyond registration fees to reduce the financial burden on families and enhance what we can offer riders.
You'll develop sponsor relationships, create sponsorship packages, organize fundraising events, and pursue grants.

middle.name: Lehi Jr
middle.notes: 6th grader open house night — potential tabling opportunity

segments.title: Introductions + Favorite MTB Moment
segments.description: We'll go around the room. If you're new, introduce yourself. Everyone shares one favorite MTB moment from last season — keep it to a sentence or two. It doesn't have to be impressive. It just has to be real.

segments.title: {{priorSeasonYear}} Season — What Worked
segments.description: Quick look back before we look forward. We'll highlight a few things that worked well and name one or two things we're building on this season.

segments.title: Role Assignments
segments.description: This is the heart of the meeting. We'll work through all roles using the worksheets you completed ahead of time. Each role comes with real ownership and the freedom to build it out the way you want. If anything's still open at the end, we'll button it up within 48 hours.

segments.title: School Outreach Assignments
segments.description: We're split into two groups this year — elementary and middle schools — because the approach and materials differ between them. Each coach will own 1–2 schools. Your worksheet will have flagged any existing connections, which helps a lot here.

segments.title: Parent Kickoff Meeting
segments.description: We need to get a date on the calendar for the parent kickoff — ideally before registration opens April 1. We'll decide together on timing, format, and who coordinates it. The goal here is just to leave with a date agreed and an owner assigned.

segments.title: {{year}} Recruiting Materials
segments.description: Quick look at the {{year}} yard signs and posters so everyone knows what we've got. These are the materials that go with your school outreach assignment, so we want you to see them before you walk out the door.

segments.title: What's Next
segments.description: We'll close by going around the room one more time. Each coach shares their role, their school(s), and their first action item. Keep it quick. Monthly check-ins through the pre-season will keep us all coordinated without a lot of overhead.

materials: School outreach list with PTA contacts
